scenario: Chronic lumbar spondylosis — DSP permanent impairment

guidedAnswers.clinicalNarrative: Severe lumbar spondylosis with multilevel disc disease and bilateral foraminal stenosis. Patient has been managed conservatively for 8 years with inadequate pain relief. Unable to stand or walk for more than 10 minutes. Specialist spinal surgeon has assessed as permanent and not amenable to further surgical intervention.

clinicalNarrative: Patricia Nguyen has severe lumbar spondylosis with multilevel disc disease confirmed on MRI. She has been under specialist spinal care for 8 years. She is unable to stand or walk for more than 10 minutes and requires a walking aid. All conservative and surgical options have been exhausted. Condition is permanent and not improving. She requires daily assistance with personal care and household tasks.
